_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to do a full investigation — ask a question, collect data, show it and explain what we found.

_source.w: We are learning to do a full investigation — ask a question, collect data, show it and explain what we found.
y: Planning a full investigation builds independence and connects all our data skills together.
This is how real researchers work — they ask, investigate, display and explain.
Learning to draw conclusions from data is one of the most important skills we develop in school.
eg: Investigating: 'What is the most common eye colour in our class?'
Planning what data to collect, how to collect it and how to display it.
Drawing conclusions: 'Brown eyes are the most common because 12 out of 25 students have brown eyes.'
Presenting our investigation findings to the class using a poster and graph.
Reflecting on whether our question was a good one and what we would change next time.
Comparing our findings with another class and discussing the differences.
